🧪 Catalog Quality Audit

Generated 2026-04-22T03:58:39.162022+00:00. This is the “show me the weird stuff” dashboard: failed taxonomy, CLIP behavior, source overlap, brand weirdness, and product evidence.

🚑 How we get failed numbers down

  1. Review the failed bucket first
    0 products are in Other/Failed. Gold-labeling the top brand/token clusters will reduce visible failures fastest.
  2. Refresh CLIP coverage after big scrapes
    0 products have no matching CLIP audit row. Those cannot benefit from the vision fallback until `vision_category_audit.full.json` is regenerated.
  3. Prefer source-backed rules over broad keyword guessing
    Use reliable source fields like Fresh Produce, retailer breadcrumbs, and product-detail metadata before adding keyword rules that can move drinks, supplements, or personal care into the wrong shelf.
  4. Use gold labels as training data
    Pick representative products from repeated failed brands and product families. One good label can help a whole cluster through the local text model.
